messages.content:
You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

Never modify files outside of the current working directory unless requested.

Current directory: ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Fetch Tool

- Use repo's README `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openai/evals/refs/heads/main/README.md` instead of `https://github.com/openai/evals`.
- Prefer raw content like `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g0t4/dotfiles/refs/heads/master/install/symlinks.fish` instead of `https://github.com/g0t4/dotfiles/blob/master/install/symlinks.fish`
- When relevant, prefer github CLI: `gh pr list` or `gh issue view 12`


## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="qwen3.6-35b-a3b<wes.mcclure+qwen3.6-35b-a3b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.

## General Code Preferences

- Avoid ambiguity, for example:

```lua
-- 👎 if there's a bug in the regex, how would I know? I'd have to surmise from surrounding code 🤮
if line:match("^%s*$") then
    -- ...
end
```

```lua
-- 🙌 clear intent
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

- use snake_case for functions and variables
- use PascalCase for classes
- use EmmyLua/LuaLS annotations to improve code completion
- use `luac` to check for syntax errors

Examples:
```lua
---@param names string[]
---@param checker fun(name: string): boolean
---@return table<string, integer>
function process_names(names, checker) end

---@class WindowController
---@field window hs.axuielement
---@field title? string
---@field size { w: number, h: number }
local WindowController = {}
```

# Semantic Grep matches: 5

This is automatic context based on my request. These may or may not be relevant.
## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rewrites/frontend.lua:0-19
local buffers = require("ask-openai.helpers.buffers")
local config = require("ask-openai.config")
local curl = require("ask-openai.backends.curl")
local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local agentica = require("ask-openai.backends.models.agentica")
local text_helpers = require("ask-openai.helpers.text")
local thinking = require("ask-openai.rewrites.thinking")
local Selection = require("ask-openai.helpers.selection")
local Displayer = require("ask-openai.rewrites.displayer")
local CurrentContext = require("ask-openai.frontends.context")
local TxChatMessage = require("ask-openai.agents.messages.tx")
local files = require("ask-openai.helpers.files")
local api = require("ask-openai.api")
local rag_client = require("ask-openai.rag.client")
local CurlRequest = require("ask-openai.backends.curl_request")
local human = require("devtools.humanize")
local tool_router = require("ask-openai.tools.router")
local model_params = require("ask-openai.agents.models.params")
local MessageBuilder = require("ask-openai.rewrites.message_builder")
local HLGroups = require("ask-openai.hlgroups")


## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/init.lua:127-137
function M.ask_openai()
    local cmdline = vim.fn.getcmdline()
    print("asking...") -- overwrites showing luaeval("...") in cmdline

    local stdin_text = ' env: nvim (neovim) command mode (return a valid command w/o the leading : ) \n question: ' ..
        cmdline

    local suggest = require("ask-openai.cmdline.suggest")
    local result = suggest.get_vim_command_suggestion(stdin_text)
    return trim_null_characters(result)
end

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/api.lua:0-5
local M = {}
local init = require("ask-openai")
local config = require("ask-openai.config")
local lualine = require('ask-openai.status.lualine')
local LlamaServerClient = require('ask-openai.backends.llama_cpp.llama_server_client')



## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/frontends/instructs.lua:0-12
local log = require("ask-openai.logs.logger").predictions()
local files = require("ask-openai.helpers.files")

-- global instructs: `~/.agents/instructs`
-- per repo: `.agents/instructs`

local M = {}

-- Cache of instruct name -> absolute path of the instruct markdown file
M._instruct_paths_by_name = {}
-- Cache of instruct name -> instruct md contents
M._instruct_contents_by_name = {}



## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/rewrites/thinking/init.lua:0-6
local log = require('ask-openai.logs.logger').predictions()
local harmony = require('ask-openai.backends.models.gptoss.tokenizer').harmony

local M = {
    dots = require("ask-openai.frontends.thinking.dots"),
}
what is the date and what is my hostname

messages.reasoning_content: The user wants to know the current date and their hostname. I can get this information using shell commands.
The user asked for the date and hostname. I have the information from the shell command output.

function.name: fetch

start_index.description: Start at this character offset, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required

max_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to return

raw.description: Do not convert HTML to markdown

function.description: Fetch a URL and extract HTML contents as markdown

function.name: delegate

agent_type.description: which subagent profile to use, options: assistant, web-researcher, command-runner, file-finder, test-finder

recursion_limit.description: LangChain's max messages for the subagent before stopping. Each subagent tool call produces 2 messages (input + output). Leave unset unless the task needs more or fewer than the default (50).

description.description: describe the task for the agent to perform

function.description: Delegate to a subagent to perform relevent tasks and summarize findings.

function.name: count
function.description: Count to, for testing progress and cancellation

function.name: run_process

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine

function.name: semantic_grep
function.description: Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.

embed_top_k.description: number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking

query.description: query text, what you are looking for

filetype.description: limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes

top_k.description: number of matches to return (post reranking)

instruct.description: instructions for the query, explain the type of query

function.name: run_in_neovim
function.description: Execute Lua code in the current Neovim process (that hosts your agent). For example, call vim.cmd()

lua.description: Lua code to execute. Should be a valid Lua chunk.

generation_settings.grammar:
boolean ::= ("true" | "false") space
char ::= [^"\\x7F\x00-\x1F] | [\] (["\bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4})
decimal-part ::= [0-9]{1,16}
integer ::= ("-"? integral-part) space
integral-part ::= [0] | [1-9] [0-9]{0,15}
json-array ::= "[" space ("]" | json-value ("," space json-value)* space "]") space
json-bool ::= ("true" | "false") space
json-null ::= "null" space
json-number ::= "-"? ("0" | [1-9] [0-9]*) ("." [0-9]+)? (("e" | "E") [+-]? [0-9]+)? space
json-object ::= "{" space ("}" | json-string space ":" space json-value (space "," space json-string space ":" space json-value)* space "}") space
json-string ::= """ ( [^"\] | "\" ( ["\/ bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4} ) )* """ space
json-value ::= json-object | json-array | json-string | json-number | json-bool | json-null
number ::= ("-"? integral-part) ("." decimal-part)? ([eE] [-+]? integral-part)? space
root ::= tool-call
space ::= | " " | "
"{1,2} [ 	]{0,20}
string ::= """ char* """ space
tool-call ::= "<tool_call>
" space (tool-fetch | tool-delegate | tool-count | tool-run-process | tool-semantic-grep | tool-run-in-neovim) space "</tool_call>" (space "<tool_call>
" space (tool-fetch | tool-delegate | tool-count | tool-run-process | tool-semantic-grep | tool-run-in-neovim) space "</tool_call>")* space
tool-count ::= ("<function=" "count" ">
") space tool-count-arg-to space "</function>
"
tool-count-arg-to ::= ("<parameter=" "to" ">
") "" integer space "</parameter>
"
tool-delegate ::= ("<function=" "delegate" ">
") space tool-delegate-arg-description (space (tool-delegate-arg-agent-type | tool-delegate-arg-recursion-limit))* space "</function>
"
tool-delegate-arg-agent-type ::= ("<parameter=" "agent_type" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-delegate-arg-description ::= ("<parameter=" "description" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-delegate-arg-recursion-limit ::= ("<parameter=" "recursion_limit" ">
") "" integer space "</parameter>
"
tool-fetch ::= ("<function=" "fetch" ">
") space tool-fetch-arg-url (space (tool-fetch-arg-start-index | tool-fetch-arg-max-chars | tool-fetch-arg-raw))* space "</function>
"
tool-fetch-arg-max-chars ::= ("<parameter=" "max_chars" ">
") "" integer space "</parameter>
"
tool-fetch-arg-raw ::= ("<parameter=" "raw" ">
") "" boolean space "</parameter>
"
tool-fetch-arg-start-index ::= ("<parameter=" "start_index" ">
") "" integer space "</parameter>
"
tool-fetch-arg-url ::= ("<parameter=" "url" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-run-in-neovim ::= ("<function=" "run_in_neovim" ">
") space tool-run-in-neovim-arg-lua space "</function>
"
tool-run-in-neovim-arg-lua ::= ("<parameter=" "lua" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-run-process ::= ("<function=" "run_process" ">
") space (space (tool-run-process-arg-cwd | tool-run-process-arg-stdin-text | tool-run-process-arg-command-line | tool-run-process-arg-timeout-ms | tool-run-process-arg-argv))* space "</function>
"
tool-run-process-arg-argv ::= ("<parameter=" "argv" ">
") "" tool-run-process-arg-argv-schema space "</parameter>
"
tool-run-process-arg-argv-schema ::= "[" space string ("," space string)* "]" space
tool-run-process-arg-command-line ::= ("<parameter=" "command_line" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-run-process-arg-cwd ::= ("<parameter=" "cwd" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-run-process-arg-stdin-text ::= ("<parameter=" "stdin_text" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-run-process-arg-timeout-ms ::= ("<parameter=" "timeout_ms" ">
") "" number space "</parameter>
"
tool-semantic-grep ::= ("<function=" "semantic_grep" ">
") space tool-semantic-grep-arg-query (space (tool-semantic-grep-arg-embed-top-k | tool-semantic-grep-arg-filetype | tool-semantic-grep-arg-top-k | tool-semantic-grep-arg-instruct))* space "</function>
"
tool-semantic-grep-arg-embed-top-k ::= ("<parameter=" "embed_top_k" ">
") "" number space "</parameter>
"
tool-semantic-grep-arg-filetype ::= ("<parameter=" "filetype" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-semantic-grep-arg-instruct ::= ("<parameter=" "instruct" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-semantic-grep-arg-query ::= ("<parameter=" "query" ">
") "" until-suffix "</parameter>
"
tool-semantic-grep-arg-top-k ::= ("<parameter=" "top_k" ">
") "" number space "</parameter>
"
until-suffix ::= ([^<] | "<" [^/] | "</" [^p] | "</p" [^a] | "</pa" [^r] | "</par" [^a] | "</para" [^m] | "</param" [^e] | "</parame" [^t] | "</paramet" [^e] | "</paramete" [^r] | "</parameter" [^>] | "</parameter>" [^
])*

__verbose.prompt:
<|im_start|>system
# Tools

You have access to the following functions:

<tools>
{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "fetch", "description": "Fetch a URL and extract HTML contents as markdown", "parameters": {"properties": {"url": {"type": "string"}, "start_index": {"default": 0, "type": "integer", "description": "Start at this character offset, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required"}, "max_chars": {"default": 50000, "type": "integer", "description": "Maximum number of characters to return"}, "raw": {"default": false, "type": "boolean", "description": "Do not convert HTML to markdown"}}, "required": ["url"], "type": "object"}}}
{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "delegate", "description": "Delegate to a subagent to perform relevent tasks and summarize findings.", "parameters": {"properties": {"agent_type": {"type": "string", "description": "which subagent profile to use, options: assistant, web-researcher, command-runner, file-finder, test-finder"}, "recursion_limit": {"type": "integer", "description": "LangChain's max messages for the subagent before stopping. Each subagent tool call produces 2 messages (input + output). Leave unset unless the task needs more or fewer than the default (50)."}, "description": {"type": "string", "description": "describe the task for the agent to perform"}}, "required": ["description"], "type": "object"}}}
{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "count", "description": "Count to, for testing progress and cancellation", "parameters": {"properties": {"to": {"type": "integer", "description": "the integer to count to"}}, "required": ["to"], "type": "object"}}}
{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "run_process", "description": "Run a process on this darwin machine", "parameters": {"type": "object", "properties": {"cwd": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional to set working directory"}, "stdin_text": {"type": "string", "description": "Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents."}, "command_line": {"type": "string", "description": "Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'."}, "timeout_ms": {"type": "number", "description": "Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms"}, "argv": {"items": {"type": "string"}, "minItems": 1, "type": "array", "description": "Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'."}}}}}
{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "semantic_grep", "description": "Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.", "parameters": {"properties": {"embed_top_k": {"type": "number", "description": "number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking"}, "query": {"type": "string", "description": "query text, what you are looking for"}, "filetype": {"type": "string", "description": "limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes"}, "top_k": {"type": "number", "description": "number of matches to return (post reranking)"}, "instruct": {"type": "string", "description": "instructions for the query, explain the type of query"}}, "type": "object", "required": ["query"]}}}
{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "run_in_neovim", "description": "Execute Lua code in the current Neovim process (that hosts your agent). For example, call vim.cmd()", "parameters": {"properties": {"lua": {"type": "string", "description": "Lua code to execute. Should be a valid Lua chunk."}}, "required": ["lua"], "type": "object"}}}
</tools>

If you choose to call a function ONLY reply in the following format with NO suffix:

<tool_call>
<function=example_function_name>
<parameter=example_parameter_1>
value_1
</parameter>
<parameter=example_parameter_2>
This is the value for the second parameter
that can span
multiple lines
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<IMPORTANT>
Reminder:
- Function calls MUST follow the specified format: an inner <function=...></function> block must be nested within <tool_call></tool_call> XML tags
- Required parameters MUST be specified
- You may provide optional reasoning for your function call in natural language BEFORE the function call, but NOT after
- If there is no function call available, answer the question like normal with your current knowledge and do not tell the user about function calls
</IMPORTANT>

<|im_start|>user
what is the date and what is my hostname<|im_end|>
<|im_start|>assistant
<think>
The user wants to know the current date and their hostname. I can get this information using shell commands.
</think>

<tool_call>
<function=run_process>
<parameter=command_line>
date && hostname
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call><|im_end|>
<|im_start|>user
<tool_response>
{"content":[{"name":"EXIT_CODE","text":"0","type":"text"},{"name":"STDOUT","text":"Fri Jun  5 18:27:16 CDT 2026\nmbp21\n","type":"text"}]}
</tool_response><|im_end|>
<|im_start|>assistant
<think>
